subject: general-knowledge
description: Ussr Russia History MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: In which year did the Russian Revolution take place?
mcqs.explanation: The Russian Revolution took place in 1917, with the February Revolution overthrowing Tsar Nicholas II and the October Revolution bringing the Bolsheviks to power.

mcqs.q: Who converted Russia from a kingdom to an empire in 1721?
mcqs.explanation: Peter the Great proclaimed the Russian Empire in 1721 after the Great Northern War. He modernized Russia and founded St. Petersburg as 'the window to Europe'.

mcqs.q: Who ruled Russia from 1762 to 1796?
mcqs.explanation: Catherine II (Catherine the Great) ruled Russia from 1762-1796, expanding Russia into Crimea, Ukraine, Poland, and the Baltic regions.

mcqs.q: Who was the notorious character with influence over the Russian royal family?
mcqs.explanation: Grigori Rasputin was a mystic who gained influence over Tsarina Alexandra and the Romanov royal family before his assassination in 1916.

mcqs.q: The March 1917 revolution against Tsar Nicholas II is known as?
mcqs.explanation: The February Revolution (March 1917 by Gregorian calendar) overthrew Tsar Nicholas II and ended the Romanov dynasty.

mcqs.q: The Bolshevik Revolution is also known as?
mcqs.explanation: The Bolshevik Revolution of November 1917 (October by Julian calendar) is known as the October Revolution or Red Revolution.

mcqs.q: Who is considered the Father of the Russian Revolution?
mcqs.explanation: Vladimir Lenin led the Bolshevik Party and the October Revolution in 1917, establishing the world's first communist state.

mcqs.q: In which country did the Bolshevik Revolution take place?
mcqs.explanation: The Bolshevik Revolution took place in Russia in October/November 1917, led by Lenin's Bolshevik Party.

mcqs.q: The USSR existed during which period?
mcqs.explanation: The Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR) was founded on 30 December 1922 and dissolved on 26 December 1991.

mcqs.q: Who translated The Communist Manifesto into Russian?
mcqs.explanation: Vladimir Lenin translated The Communist Manifesto into Russian, spreading Marxist ideology in Russia.

mcqs.q: Under whose leadership was the Soviet Union formed in 1922?
mcqs.explanation: The Soviet Union was formed on 30 December 1922 under the leadership of Vladimir Lenin after the Russian Civil War.

mcqs.q: The USSR was primarily influenced by which ideology?
mcqs.explanation: The USSR was a communist state based on Marxist-Leninist ideology, with centralized state control over economy and politics.

mcqs.q: Who was the first president of the USSR?
mcqs.explanation: Vladimir Lenin was the first leader of the USSR, serving from 1922 until his death in 1924.

mcqs.q: Who served as leader of the USSR from 1924 to 1952?
mcqs.explanation: Joseph Stalin led the USSR from 1924 after Lenin's death until his own death in 1953, during which he industrialized the USSR and led it through WWII.

mcqs.q: Who was the USSR leader during the Cuban Missile Crisis in 1962?
mcqs.explanation: Nikita Khrushchev led the USSR during the Cuban Missile Crisis of October 1962, the closest the world came to nuclear war.

mcqs.q: Nikita Khrushchev served as USSR leader from?
mcqs.explanation: Nikita Khrushchev led the USSR from 1953 (formally 1952) to 1964, introducing De-Stalinization policies.

mcqs.q: Leonid Brezhnev served as USSR leader from?
mcqs.explanation: Leonid Brezhnev led the Soviet Union from 1964 to 1982, a period known as the 'Era of Stagnation'.

mcqs.q: Who was the last president of the USSR?
mcqs.explanation: Mikhail Gorbachev was the last Soviet leader (1985-1991), whose Perestroika and Glasnost reforms led to the dissolution of the USSR.

mcqs.q: Why are communists called 'Reds'?
mcqs.explanation: Communists are called 'Reds' because the color of the communist flag is red, symbolizing the blood of workers in class struggle.

mcqs.q: The theory of class struggle is related to?
mcqs.explanation: Karl Marx developed the theory of class struggle in his works including Das Kapital and The Communist Manifesto (co-authored with Engels).

mcqs.q: Leo Tolstoy, author of Anna Karenina and War and Peace, was from?
mcqs.explanation: Leo Tolstoy (1828-1910) was a Russian novelist and one of the greatest writers of all time, famous for War and Peace and Anna Karenina.

mcqs.q: The Lenin Prize was started in which year?
mcqs.explanation: The Lenin Prize was established in 1925 to honor outstanding achievements in science, literature, arts, and other fields.

mcqs.q: What was the purpose of the Marshall Plan in 1948?
mcqs.explanation: The Marshall Plan (1948) was an American initiative to provide economic aid to Western European countries after WWII to prevent communist influence.

mcqs.q: The treaty signed in 1949 to counter USSR forces was?
mcqs.explanation: NATO (North Atlantic Treaty Organization) was established on 4 April 1949 via the Washington Treaty as a collective defense alliance against Soviet expansion.

mcqs.q: Which country was the host member of the Warsaw Pact?
mcqs.explanation: The Warsaw Pact was signed in Warsaw, Poland in 1955, making Poland the host country of the military alliance.

mcqs.q: Russia signed the Warsaw Pact in which year?
mcqs.explanation: The Warsaw Pact was signed on 14 May 1955 by the USSR and seven Eastern European communist states as a response to NATO.

mcqs.q: The Warsaw Pact was dissolved in which year?
mcqs.explanation: The Warsaw Pact was dissolved on 1 July 1991, following the collapse of communism in Eastern Europe.

mcqs.q: The Berlin Wall was created in August 1961 to?
mcqs.explanation: The Berlin Wall was built by East Germany in August 1961 to prevent its population from fleeing to West Berlin and the capitalist West.

mcqs.q: The term 'Iron Curtain' was popularized by?
mcqs.explanation: British PM Winston Churchill popularized the term 'Iron Curtain' in his March 1946 speech at Fulton, Missouri, describing the division of Europe.

mcqs.q: In December 1979, which country did the Soviet Union invade?
mcqs.explanation: The Soviet Union invaded Afghanistan on 24 December 1979, beginning a nine-year war that contributed to the USSR's eventual collapse.

mcqs.q: The Geneva Accord was signed during the tenure of which Russian president?
mcqs.explanation: The Geneva Accords (1988) on Afghanistan were signed during Mikhail Gorbachev's tenure, leading to Soviet withdrawal from Afghanistan by 1989.

mcqs.q: In which year was the Geneva Accord signed?
mcqs.explanation: The Geneva Accords were signed on 14 April 1988 between Afghanistan, Pakistan, USA and USSR, ending the Soviet-Afghan War.

mcqs.q: Who was the Prime Minister of the USSR during the Tashkent Agreement?
mcqs.explanation: Alexei Kosygin was the Soviet Premier who mediated the Tashkent Declaration signed in January 1966 between India and Pakistan.

mcqs.q: Who started the reforms called Perestroika and Glasnost in 1985?
mcqs.explanation: Mikhail Gorbachev launched Perestroika (restructuring) and Glasnost (openness) in 1985 to reform the Soviet political and economic system.

mcqs.q: The Soviet Union was replaced by the Russian Federation in?
mcqs.explanation: The USSR was formally dissolved on 26 December 1991, and the Russian Federation emerged as its main successor state.

mcqs.q: The Commonwealth of Independent States (CIS) was established on?
mcqs.explanation: The CIS was established on 8 December 1991 via the Belovezha Accords signed by Russia, Ukraine, and Belarus.

mcqs.q: The treaty associated with the dissolution of the USSR (1991) is?
mcqs.explanation: The Belovezha Accords, signed on 8 December 1991, formally dissolved the Soviet Union and created the CIS.

mcqs.q: The primary purpose of the Warsaw Pact (1955) was?
mcqs.explanation: The Warsaw Pact was a military alliance among the USSR and Eastern Bloc communist countries, created as a counterweight to NATO.

mcqs.q: Which was NOT a founding republic of the Soviet Union in 1922?
mcqs.explanation: The four founding republics of the USSR in 1922 were Russia, Ukraine, Belarus, and the Transcaucasian SFSR. Armenia joined later as part of Transcaucasia.

mcqs.q: Who initiated the policy of 'De-Stalinization' in the 1950s?
mcqs.explanation: Nikita Khrushchev began De-Stalinization after his 1956 'Secret Speech' denouncing Stalin's crimes and personality cult.

mcqs.q: The USSR disintegrated into how many independent states in 1991?
mcqs.explanation: The USSR disintegrated into 15 independent republics in 1991, including Russia, Ukraine, Belarus, Kazakhstan, and the Baltic states.

mcqs.q: Chechnya declared independence from the USSR in?
mcqs.explanation: Chechnya declared independence from the USSR in 1991, leading to the First Chechen War (1994-1996) with Russia.

mcqs.q: Russia annexed the Crimean Peninsula from Ukraine in?
mcqs.explanation: Russia annexed Crimea from Ukraine in March 2014 after a controversial referendum, following the ousting of Ukrainian President Yanukovych.

mcqs.q: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky was previously a?
mcqs.explanation: Volodymyr Zelensky was a popular comedian and actor before becoming President of Ukraine in 2019. He played a president in the TV series 'Servant of the People'.

mcqs.q: In which city is St. Basil's Cathedral located?
mcqs.explanation: St. Basil's Cathedral, with its iconic colorful onion domes, is located in Red Square, Moscow. It was built by Ivan the Terrible in 1555-1561.

mcqs.q: The Open Skies Treaty was signed in?
mcqs.explanation: The Open Skies Treaty was signed on 24 March 1992, allowing participating states to conduct unarmed aerial surveillance flights over each other's territories.

mcqs.q: The Zircon hypersonic cruise missile was tested by?
mcqs.explanation: The Zircon (Tsirkon) is a hypersonic cruise missile developed and tested by Russia, capable of speeds up to Mach 9.

mcqs.q: The Chernobyl nuclear incident occurred in?
mcqs.explanation: The Chernobyl nuclear disaster occurred on 26 April 1986 at the Chernobyl Nuclear Power Plant in Ukrainian SSR, the worst nuclear accident in history.

mcqs.q: The first president of the Russian Federation was?
mcqs.explanation: Boris Yeltsin was the first President of the Russian Federation, serving from 1991 to 1999 after the dissolution of the USSR.

mcqs.q: Russia attacked and annexed territory from Georgia in?
mcqs.explanation: Russia fought a brief war with Georgia in August 2008 and subsequently recognized the independence of South Ossetia and Abkhazia.

mcqs.q: The famous space station in Kazakhstan originally built by the USSR is?
mcqs.explanation: Baikonur Cosmodrome in Kazakhstan was built by the USSR in 1955 and is the world's first and largest operational space launch facility.

mcqs.q: Russia is the world's largest country by?
mcqs.explanation: Russia is the largest country in the world by land area, covering approximately 17.1 million sq km or about one-eighth of Earth's inhabited land.

mcqs.q: How many time zones does Russia span?
mcqs.explanation: Russia spans 11 time zones, from Kaliningrad in the west to Kamchatka in the east, reflecting its vast east-west extent.

mcqs.q: What is the capital of Russia?
mcqs.explanation: Moscow is the capital and largest city of Russia, and also Europe's largest city. It is the seat of executive, legislative, and judicial power.

mcqs.q: How many countries share land borders with Russia?
mcqs.explanation: Russia shares land borders with 14 countries: Azerbaijan, Belarus, China, Estonia, Finland, Georgia, Kazakhstan, North Korea, Latvia, Lithuania, Mongolia, Norway, Poland, and Ukraine.

mcqs.q: Which mountain range forms the boundary between European and Asian Russia?
mcqs.explanation: The Ural Mountains form the traditional continental boundary between Europe and Asia, dividing European and Asian parts of Russia.

mcqs.q: What percentage of Russia's territory does Siberia cover?
mcqs.explanation: Siberia covers about 77% of Russia's territory but holds less than one-fourth of its population, making it one of Earth's most sparsely populated major regions.

mcqs.q: Which is Europe's longest river?
mcqs.explanation: The Volga River is Europe's longest river at 3,690 km, flowing entirely through Russia and emptying into the Caspian Sea.

mcqs.q: Lake Baikal is the world's?
mcqs.explanation: Lake Baikal in Siberia is the world's deepest (1,642 m) and oldest (25+ million years) freshwater lake, holding about 20% of Earth's unfrozen freshwater.

mcqs.q: About what percentage of Earth's unfrozen freshwater does Lake Baikal hold?
mcqs.explanation: Lake Baikal holds approximately 20% of the world's unfrozen surface freshwater — more than all the North American Great Lakes combined.

mcqs.q: Which peninsula hosts over 160 volcanoes?
mcqs.explanation: The Kamchatka Peninsula hosts over 160 volcanoes, around 29 active, making it one of Earth's most volcanically active regions. It is a UNESCO World Heritage site.

mcqs.q: Oymyakon in Sakha Republic is known as?
mcqs.explanation: Oymyakon is the coldest permanently inhabited place on Earth, recording -71.2°C in 1924.

mcqs.q: About what percentage of Earth's forests are in Russia?
mcqs.explanation: About 20% of Earth's forests are in Russia, making it the largest forested nation in the world. The vast taiga forest dominates Siberia.

mcqs.q: The Kremlin is located in?
mcqs.explanation: The Kremlin is a historic fortified citadel in Moscow, founded in the 12th century and serving as the seat of Russian political power.

mcqs.q: Russia has coastlines on which oceans?
mcqs.explanation: Russia is the only country with coastlines on three oceans — Arctic, Pacific, and Atlantic (via the Baltic and Black Seas).

mcqs.q: The Kaliningrad exclave is bordered by?
mcqs.explanation: Kaliningrad is a Russian exclave on the Baltic Sea, bordered by Poland and Lithuania, separated from mainland Russia.

mcqs.q: What is the form of government in Russia?
mcqs.explanation: Russia is a federal semi-presidential republic, though critics argue it functions as an authoritarian state under strong presidential dominance.

mcqs.q: What is the upper house of the Russian Parliament called?
mcqs.explanation: The Federation Council is the upper house of Russia's Federal Assembly, representing the 85 federal subjects (regions) of Russia.

mcqs.q: What is the lower house of the Russian Parliament called?
mcqs.explanation: The State Duma is the lower house of Russia's Federal Assembly, with 450 members elected to five-year terms, responsible for legislation.

mcqs.q: When was Russia's current Constitution adopted?
mcqs.explanation: The Constitution of Russia was adopted on 12 December 1993 by national referendum and amended in 2020 to expand presidential authority.

mcqs.q: What is Russia's ruling political party?
mcqs.explanation: United Russia is the dominant ruling party of Russia, supporting President Vladimir Putin's policies.

mcqs.q: What is Russia's national currency?
mcqs.explanation: The Russian Ruble (RUB) is the official currency of the Russian Federation, divided into 100 kopeks.

mcqs.q: What is Russia's national emblem?
mcqs.explanation: The double-headed eagle is Russia's national emblem, symbolizing the unity of East and West. It was restored in 1993 after the Soviet era.

mcqs.q: Who composed Russia's national anthem?
mcqs.explanation: Alexander Alexandrov composed the music of 'Gosudarstvenny Gimn Rossiyskoy Federatsii', originally the Soviet anthem, reinstated as Russia's anthem in 2000.

mcqs.q: How many federal subjects does Russia have?
mcqs.explanation: Russia is composed of 85 federal subjects: 46 oblasts, 22 republics, 9 krais, 3 federal cities, 1 autonomous oblast, and 4 autonomous okrugs.

mcqs.q: Russia holds the world's largest reserves of?
mcqs.explanation: Russia holds the world's largest natural gas reserves and the second-largest coal reserves, making it a major energy exporter.

mcqs.q: Which is Russia's state-controlled natural gas company?
mcqs.explanation: Gazprom is Russia's state-controlled natural gas giant, holding some of the world's largest gas reserves and dominant in European gas supply.

mcqs.q: Russia is a founding member of which international group?
mcqs.explanation: Russia is a founding member of BRICS (Brazil, Russia, India, China, South Africa) and the Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO).

mcqs.q: Russia's space agency is known as?
mcqs.explanation: Roscosmos is Russia's state corporation responsible for space operations, successor to the Soviet space program that launched Sputnik in 1957.

mcqs.q: What is Russia's official language?
mcqs.explanation: Russian is the official language of the Russian Federation and the most spoken native language in Europe with over 150 million native speakers.

mcqs.q: What is Russia's largest religion?
mcqs.explanation: Russian Orthodox Christianity is the largest religion in Russia, with Islam as the second largest. Other religions include Buddhism and Judaism.

mcqs.q: Russia's population in 2025 is approximately?
mcqs.explanation: Russia's population in 2025 is approximately 144 million, ranking 9th in the world. About 80% are ethnic Russians.

mcqs.q: Who has dominated Russian politics since 1999?
mcqs.explanation: Vladimir Putin has dominated Russian politics since 1999, serving alternately as President and Prime Minister of Russia.

mcqs.q: Ivan IV, who became the first Tsar in 1547, was also known as?
mcqs.explanation: Ivan IV (1530-1584) became the first Tsar of Russia in 1547 and is known as Ivan the Terrible for his harsh rule and military expansion.

mcqs.q: Peter the Great founded which city?
mcqs.explanation: Peter the Great founded Saint Petersburg in 1703 as Russia's 'window to Europe' and made it the capital of the Russian Empire.

mcqs.q: The Mongol invasion subjugated the Rus' principalities starting from?
mcqs.explanation: The Mongol invasion led by Batu Khan subjugated the Rus' principalities starting in 1240 and lasted for over 200 years (the 'Tatar Yoke').

mcqs.q: Kievan Rus' adopted Christianity in which year?
mcqs.explanation: Kievan Rus' adopted Christianity in 988 AD under Prince Vladimir the Great, marking the beginning of Russian Orthodox Christianity.

mcqs.q: Kievan Rus' was founded in the 9th century by?
mcqs.explanation: Kievan Rus' was founded in the 9th century by Varangians (Scandinavian Vikings) led by Rurik, establishing the first East Slavic state.

mcqs.q: The Soviet Union became a superpower after?
mcqs.explanation: The Soviet Union emerged as a superpower after World War II, rivaling the United States during the Cold War (1947-1991).

mcqs.q: The Cold War lasted from?
mcqs.explanation: The Cold War was the ideological conflict between the USA and USSR from approximately 1947 (Truman Doctrine) to 1991 (USSR dissolution).

mcqs.q: The Berlin Wall fell in?
mcqs.explanation: The Berlin Wall fell on 9 November 1989, symbolizing the end of the Cold War and paving the way for German reunification in 1990.

mcqs.q: The Cuban Missile Crisis occurred in?
mcqs.explanation: The Cuban Missile Crisis occurred in October 1962, lasting 13 days, and brought the world to the brink of nuclear war between USA and USSR.

mcqs.q: Russia's military budget ranks globally at?
mcqs.explanation: Russia's military budget (2026) ranks 4th globally at approximately $110 billion, behind USA, China, and India.

mcqs.q: Russia's total land area is approximately?
mcqs.explanation: Russia covers approximately 17.1 million sq km, about one-eighth of Earth's inhabited land surface, making it the world's largest country.

mcqs.q: How many active volcanoes are on Kamchatka Peninsula?
mcqs.explanation: The Kamchatka Peninsula has over 160 volcanoes, of which about 29 are currently active.

mcqs.q: The Volga River length is?
mcqs.explanation: The Volga River is 3,690 km long, making it Europe's longest river. It flows through Central Russia and into the Caspian Sea.

mcqs.q: Russia's coastline length is approximately?
mcqs.explanation: Russia's coastline is approximately 37,650 km long, stretching from Murmansk on the Barents Sea to Vladivostok on the Pacific Ocean.

mcqs.q: What percentage of Russians are ethnic Russians?
mcqs.explanation: About 80% of Russia's population is ethnic Russian, with minorities including Tatars, Ukrainians, Bashkirs, Chechens, and Chuvash.

mcqs.q: What is Russia's literacy rate?
mcqs.explanation: Russia's literacy rate is approximately 99.7%, among the highest in the world, reflecting strong investment in education since Soviet times.

mcqs.q: The Kremlin was founded in which century?
mcqs.explanation: The Moscow Kremlin was founded in the 12th century, with its current brick walls built under Ivan III in the late 15th century.

mcqs.q: Russia's second-largest city is?
mcqs.explanation: Saint Petersburg, founded by Peter the Great in 1703, is Russia's second-largest city and former imperial capital.

mcqs.q: Russia shares maritime boundaries with?
mcqs.explanation: Russia has maritime boundaries with the United States (across the Bering Strait) and Japan (in the Sea of Okhotsk).

mcqs.q: The Russian Empire existed during which period?
mcqs.explanation: The Russian Empire existed from 1721 (when Peter the Great proclaimed it) until 1917 (when the Russian Revolution ended the Romanov dynasty).

mcqs.q: The Romanov monarchy ended due to?
mcqs.explanation: The Romanov dynasty (1613-1917) ended with the February Revolution of 1917, which forced Tsar Nicholas II to abdicate.

mcqs.q: The Warsaw Pact member countries were mostly from?
mcqs.explanation: Warsaw Pact members were USSR and Eastern European communist states: Poland, East Germany, Czechoslovakia, Hungary, Romania, Bulgaria, and Albania.

mcqs.q: The Soviet-Afghan War lasted from?
mcqs.explanation: The Soviet-Afghan War lasted from December 1979 to February 1989, when Soviet troops withdrew under the Geneva Accords.

mcqs.q: The Bosnia & Herzegovina referendum for independence occurred in?
mcqs.explanation: Bosnia and Herzegovina held its independence referendum in March 1992, triggering the Bosnian War (1992-1995).

mcqs.q: Boris Yeltsin served as Russian president from?
mcqs.explanation: Boris Yeltsin was the first President of the Russian Federation, serving from July 1991 to 31 December 1999, when he resigned in favor of Putin.

mcqs.q: Russia's Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ) covers approximately?
mcqs.explanation: Russia's EEZ covers approximately 7.6 million sq km, placing it among the three largest EEZs in the world.

mcqs.q: Russia's land borders total approximately?
mcqs.explanation: Russia's land borders total approximately 22,407 km, the second-longest land borders in the world after China.

mcqs.q: Lake Karachay in the southern Urals is known for?
mcqs.explanation: Lake Karachay is one of the most radioactive places on Earth due to Soviet-era nuclear waste disposal from the Mayak nuclear facility.

mcqs.q: Russia's GDP (nominal 2025) rank is approximately?
mcqs.explanation: Russia's nominal GDP in 2025 is approximately $2 trillion, ranking 10th globally.

mcqs.q: Lenin died in which year?
mcqs.explanation: Vladimir Lenin died on 21 January 1924, after which Joseph Stalin emerged as the dominant Soviet leader.

mcqs.q: Stalin led the USSR through which war?
mcqs.explanation: Joseph Stalin led the USSR through World War II (Great Patriotic War 1941-1945), defeating Nazi Germany at huge human cost.

mcqs.q: The five-year plans of the USSR were introduced by?
mcqs.explanation: Stalin introduced the Five-Year Plans starting in 1928 to rapidly industrialize and collectivize the Soviet economy.

mcqs.q: The USSR launched Sputnik, the first artificial satellite, in?
mcqs.explanation: The USSR launched Sputnik 1, the world's first artificial satellite, on 4 October 1957, starting the Space Race with the USA.

mcqs.q: The first human in space was?
mcqs.explanation: Yuri Gagarin, a Soviet cosmonaut, became the first human in space on 12 April 1961 aboard Vostok 1.

mcqs.q: The first woman in space was?
mcqs.explanation: Valentina Tereshkova, a Soviet cosmonaut, became the first woman in space on 16 June 1963 aboard Vostok 6.

mcqs.q: What does 'Perestroika' mean in English?
mcqs.explanation: Perestroika means 'restructuring' in Russian and refers to Gorbachev's 1985 economic and political reform program.

mcqs.q: What does 'Glasnost' mean in English?
mcqs.explanation: Glasnost means 'openness' or 'transparency' in Russian and refers to Gorbachev's policy of increased freedom of speech and information.

mcqs.q: The USSR's famous secret police organization was?
mcqs.explanation: The KGB (Komitet Gosudarstvennoy Bezopasnosti) was the Soviet Union's main security agency from 1954 to 1991, handling foreign intelligence and internal security.

mcqs.q: Russia holds which rank in terms of coal reserves?
mcqs.explanation: Russia holds the world's second-largest coal reserves, after the United States, and is a major coal exporter.
